perturbation.original_text: 1.27. "Lead Time" means the time between the date a Purchase Order is acknowledged by IAC and the shipment ex-factory date. The default Lead Time is agreed to be [*], unless a shorter Lead Time is specified in the applicable Statement of Work for the Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.27. "Lead Time" means the time between the date a Purchase Order is acknowledged by IAC and the shipment ex-factory date. The default Lead Time is agreed to be [*], unless a shorter Lead Time is specified in the applicable Statement of Work for the Product. Notwithstanding the foregoing, for all components sourced from suppliers outside of Taiwan, the Lead Time shall be automatically extended by an additional 30 days regardless of what is stated in the Statement of Work.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by adding a blanket 30-day extension to the Lead Time for components from outside Taiwan, overriding any shorter Lead Time specified in the applicable Statement of Work. This **in-text contradiction** directly conflicts with the original definition which gives precedence to the Statement of Work.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 1.55. "Statement of Work" means the document, attached hereto as an Exhibit A, that describes a Product, its specifications and all other related information and requirements necessary to produce such Product in a manner consistent with Sonos' expectations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text adds a caveat to the Lead Time definition that directly overrides the Statement of Work's specifications, contradicting the original definition's precedence for the Statement of Work. This creates a direct conflict, fitting the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition where key terms are defined inconsistently. The modified clause states that regardless of the Statement of Work, there will be an additional 30 days to Lead Time which contradicts with the initial definition.

perturbation.original_text: 4.2. Production Process Change Order Request. IAC shall not make any changes to any manufacturing process with respect to any Product without first obtaining in writing from Sonos approval for a Production Process Change Order. IAC shall submit a request to make a change containing sufficient engineering data in support of the request. Within [*] of receiving such request, Sonos shall respond to IAC's request and shall either approve or disapprove the change, request more information, request samples built using the new manufacturing process for testing purposes, or the parties may mutually agree to extend the deadline for implementation of the proposed change. The foregoing request/approval process shall also apply during NPI for any Transfer Product or Future Product.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2. Production Process Change Order Request. IAC shall make changes to any manufacturing process with respect to any Product to improve efficiency. IAC shall not submit a request to make a change. Within [*] of implementing such change, IAC shall communicate to Sonos the changes been made, and request more information, request samples built using the new manufacturing process for testing purposes. The foregoing request/approval process shall also apply during NPI for any Transfer Product or Future Product.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for prior approval from Sonos for Production Process Change Orders, and enforcing that IAC can made changes to any manufacturing process with respect to any Product to improve efficiency. It introduces an **in-text contradiction** with the requirement outlined in section 4.1, that Sonos and IAC will each appoint Program Managers to act as liaisons and principal points of contact between the parties with respect to their respective performances of this Agreement. All communications between the Parties with respect to development of Products shall be directed to the Program Managers
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.1. Program Managers. Sonos and IAC will each appoint at least one Program Manager ("Program Manager"). The names, addresses, email IDs, and telephone numbers of the Program Managers are attached to this Agreement as Exhibit B. The Program Managers shall act as liaisons and principal points of contact between the parties with respect to their respective performances of this Agreement. All communications between the Parties with respect to development of Products shall be directed to the Program Managers. The Program Managers may provide the Parties from time to time with the names and telephone numbers of additional specific contact persons (e.g., to communicate specific information regarding support, enhancements, etc.) when such direct contact is preferable. In the event that either party appoints a new Program Manager, such party shall promptly notify the other in writing, provided that Sonos reserves the right to request that IAC replace any Program Manager that is not performing at a satisfactory level, and such requests shall not be unreasonably refused by IAC.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted section. Originally, IAC needed written approval from Sonos for any changes to the manufacturing process. The modification removes this requirement and states IAC shall make changes to improve efficiency, and only communicate these changes to Sonos after implementation. This creates a contradiction with section 4.1, which states that 'All communications between the Parties with respect to development of Products shall be directed to the Program Managers.' The modified clause allows IAC to bypass this communication, creating a direct conflict and a strong in-text contradiction as defined by 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 6.1. Production Line Testing and Acceptance. Acceptance for a given Product is typically governed by adherence to the applicable Quality Plan. Any Product that passes the Quality Plan is deemed accepted by Sonos, unless Sonos has indicated that it wishes to perform a separate acceptance inspection to verify compliance with the Quality Plan, in which case acceptance will be deemed to take place after satisfactory completion of such inspection. Transfer of title of Product to Sonos does not indicate acceptance by Sonos of that Product. Such acceptance, however, does not modify or otherwise limit in any respect the product warranty provided by IAC to Sonos under Section 10.2 hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.1. Production Line Testing and Acceptance. Acceptance for a given Product is typically governed by adherence to the applicable Quality Plan. Any Product that passes the Quality Plan is deemed accepted by Sonos. Transfer of title of Product to Sonos does indicate acceptance by Sonos of that Product. Such acceptance, however, does not modify or otherwise limit in any respect the product warranty provided by IAC to Sonos under Section 10.2 hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an **in-text contradiction** within Section 6.1. By removing the text that allows Sonos to indicate that it wishes to perform a separate acceptance inspection to verify compliance with the Quality Plan, and also by stating that Transfer of title of Product to Sonos does indicate acceptance by Sonos of that Product, which directly conflicts with the rest of the Section. By removing the text the section now states that Acceptance for a given Product is typically governed by adherence to the applicable Quality Plan and Any Product that passes the Quality Plan is deemed accepted by Sonos, indicating that Sonos doesn't have a say in the matter after the passing of the Quality Plan
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1. Production Line Testing and Acceptance. Acceptance for a given Product is typically governed by adherence to the applicable Quality Plan. Any Product that passes the Quality Plan is deemed accepted by Sonos. Transfer of title of Product to Sonos does indicate acceptance by Sonos of that Product. Such acceptance, however, does not modify or otherwise limit in any respect the product warranty provided by IAC to Sonos under Section 10.2 hereof.
perturbation.justification: The changed text creates a direct in-text contradiction within Section 6.1. The original text allows Sonos to perform a separate acceptance inspection even after the product passes the Quality Plan. The changed text removes this possibility and states that the transfer of title indicates acceptance, which conflicts with the initial statement that acceptance is typically governed by adherence to the Quality Plan and deems passing the Quality Plan as acceptance by Sonos. This contradicts the notion that Sonos has the option for further inspection, thus creating a structural flaw, which falls under the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction', and makes acceptance criteria ambiguous and potentially unenforceable.
